content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 19, 1992

Ann E. Muller
CALBO Consultants
Connerly & Associates, Inc.
2215 21st Street
Sacramento, CA 95818

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. G-92-164

Dear Ms. Muller:

We have received your letter of March 16, 1992, in which you
seek this agency's assistance in clarifying Section 306(a) of the
1991 Uniform Building Code involving the use of special
inspectors. Specifically, you ask whether implementation of that
statute violates the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("the Act).¹ We are unable to provide you
with the assistance you request.

The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act prohibit
public officials from making, participating in, or using their
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
they know or have reason to know they have a financial interest.
(Sections 87100; 87103.) A public official includes every natural
person who is a member, officer, employee, or consultant of a
local or state government agency. (Section 82048.) Whether
special inspectors are subject to the provisions of the Act
depends upon whether a special inspector is deemed to be a "public
official" as that term is defined in the Act. (Regulation
18700(a), copy enclosed.) For your convenience and information I
have enclosed a brochure entitled "California's Conflict of
Interest Law for Public Officials."

_____________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. G-92-164
Page 2


The Commission provides advice with respect to an
individual's duties and responsibilities under the Act. Your
letter does not indicate either that you are a public official
inquiring about your obligations under the Act, or that your
inquiry otherwise presents a question arising under the Act. (See
Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), 18329(c)(4)(F).)  My telephone call to
your office on June 18, 1992, failed to elicit any additional
information.

     I am sorry that we are unable to provide you with the help
you have requested. If you have any further questions regarding
this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.2

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                By:  Deanne Stone
                                     Counsel, Legal Division

SH:DRS:aa

Enclosures




2  Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: Whether implementation of Section 306(a) of the 1991 Uniform Building Code involving the use of special inspectors violates the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: The Commission is unable to provide assistance because the letter does not indicate that the requester is a public official inquiring about their own obligations under the Act, nor does the inquiry otherwise present a question arising under the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether the implementation of a specific building code statute regarding special inspectors violates California's conflict-of-interest laws for public officials.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the request does not meet the criteria for informal assistance—specifically, the requester did not establish that they are a public official seeking guidance on their own duties under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with clear question and conclusion sections. The FPPC explicitly states it cannot provide the requested assistance. Document date is August 19, 1992; original inquiry was March 16, 1992.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether implementation of Section 306(a) of the 1991 Uniform Building Code involving the use of special inspectors violates the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Commission is unable to provide assistance because the letter does not indicate that the requester is a public official inquiring about their own obligations under the Act, nor does the inquiry otherwise present a question arising under the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines an informal advice request from Ann Muller regarding whether special inspectors under the 1991 Uniform Building Code are subject to conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, finding the inquiry does not meet the agency's criteria for providing assistance.
